[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   # mailman post: start
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   Create a new, unpopulated mailing list.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   Usage: /usr/local/cpanel/3rdparty/mailman/bin/newlist [options] [listname [listadmin-addr [admin-password]]]
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   Options:
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       -l language
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       --language=language
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           Make the list's preferred language `language', which must be a two
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           letter language code.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       -u urlhost
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       --urlhost=urlhost
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           Gives the list's web interface host name.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       -e emailhost
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       --emailhost=emailhost
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           Gives the list's email domain name.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       -q/--quiet
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           Normally the administrator is notified by email (after a prompt) that
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           their list has been created.  This option suppresses the prompt and
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           notification.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       -a/--automate
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           This option suppresses the prompt prior to administrator notification
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           but still sends the notification.  It can be used to make newlist
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           totally non-interactive but still send the notification, assuming
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           listname, listadmin-addr and admin-password are all specified on the
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           command line.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       -h/--help
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]           Print this help text and exit.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   You can specify as many of the arguments as you want on the command line:
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   you will be prompted for the missing ones.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   Every Mailman list has two parameters which define the default host name for
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   outgoing email, and the default URL for all web interfaces.  When you
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   configured Mailman, certain defaults were calculated, but if you are running
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   multiple virtual Mailman sites, then the defaults may not be appropriate for
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   the list you are creating.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   You also specify the domain to create your new list in by typing the command
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   like so:
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       newlist --urlhost=www.mydom.ain mylist
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   where `www.mydom.ain' should be the base hostname for the URL to this virtual
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   hosts's lists.  E.g. with this setting people will view the general list
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   overviews at http://www.mydom.ain/mailman/listinfo.  Also, www.mydom.ain
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   should be a key in the VIRTUAL_HOSTS mapping in mm_cfg.py/Defaults.py if
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   the email hostname to be automatically determined.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   If you want the email hostname to be different from the one looked up by the
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   VIRTUAL_HOSTS or if urlhost is not registered in VIRTUAL_HOSTS, you can specify
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   `emailhost' like so:
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       newlist --urlhost=www.mydom.ain --emailhost=mydom.ain mylist
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   where `mydom.ain' is the mail domain name. If you don't specify emailhost but
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   urlhost is not in the virtual host list, then mm_cfg.DEFAULT_EMAIL_HOST will
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   be used for the email interface.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   For backward compatibility, you can also specify the domain to create your
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   new list in by spelling the listname like so:
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]       mylist@www.mydom.ain
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   where www.mydom.ain is used for `urlhost' but it will also be used for
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   `emailhost' if it is not found in the virtual host table. Note that
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   '--urlhost' and '--emailhost' have precedence to this notation.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   If you spell the list name as just `mylist', then the email hostname will be
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   taken from DEFAULT_EMAIL_HOST and the url will be taken from DEFAULT_URL_HOST
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   interpolated into DEFAULT_URL_PATTERN (as defined in your Defaults.py file or
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   overridden by settings in mm_cfg.py).
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   Note that listnames are forced to lowercase.
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   
[2025-07-09 03:13:34 +0000]   Illegal list name: mailman@1bc2c01b8357

[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000] # mailman post: start
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000] Create a new, unpopulated mailing list.
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000] Usage: /usr/local/cpanel/3rdparty/mailman/bin/newlist [options] [listname [listadmin-addr [admin-password]]]
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000] Options:
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]     -l language
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]     --language=language
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]         Make the list's preferred language `language', which must be a two
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]         letter language code.
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]     -u urlhost
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]     --urlhost=urlhost
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]         Gives the list's web interface host name.
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]     -e emailhost
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]     --emailhost=emailhost
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]         Gives the list's email domain name.
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]     -q/--quiet
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]         Normally the administrator is notified by email (after a prompt) that
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]         their list has been created.  This option suppresses the prompt and
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]         notification.
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]     -a/--automate
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]         This option suppresses the prompt prior to administrator notification
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]         but still sends the notification.  It can be used to make newlist
[2025-07-09 03:13:35 +0000]         totally non-interactive but still send the notification, assuming
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000]         listname, listadmin-addr and admin-password are all specified on the
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000]         command line.
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000]     -h/--help
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000]         Print this help text and exit.
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] You can specify as many of the arguments as you want on the command line:
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] you will be prompted for the missing ones.
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] Every Mailman list has two parameters which define the default host name for
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] outgoing email, and the default URL for all web interfaces.  When you
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] configured Mailman, certain defaults were calculated, but if you are running
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] multiple virtual Mailman sites, then the defaults may not be appropriate for
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] the list you are creating.
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] You also specify the domain to create your new list in by typing the command
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] like so:
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000]     newlist --urlhost=www.mydom.ain mylist
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] where `www.mydom.ain' should be the base hostname for the URL to this virtual
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] hosts's lists.  E.g. with this setting people will view the general list
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] overviews at http://www.mydom.ain/mailman/listinfo.  Also, www.mydom.ain
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] should be a key in the VIRTUAL_HOSTS mapping in mm_cfg.py/Defaults.py if
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] the email hostname to be automatically determined.
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] If you want the email hostname to be different from the one looked up by the
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] VIRTUAL_HOSTS or if urlhost is not registered in VIRTUAL_HOSTS, you can specify
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] `emailhost' like so:
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000]     newlist --urlhost=www.mydom.ain --emailhost=mydom.ain mylist
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] where `mydom.ain' is the mail domain name. If you don't specify emailhost but
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] urlhost is not in the virtual host list, then mm_cfg.DEFAULT_EMAIL_HOST will
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] be used for the email interface.
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] For backward compatibility, you can also specify the domain to create your
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] new list in by spelling the listname like so:
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000]     mylist@www.mydom.ain
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] where www.mydom.ain is used for `urlhost' but it will also be used for
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] `emailhost' if it is not found in the virtual host table. Note that
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] '--urlhost' and '--emailhost' have precedence to this notation.
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] If you spell the list name as just `mylist', then the email hostname will be
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] taken from DEFAULT_EMAIL_HOST and the url will be taken from DEFAULT_URL_HOST
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] interpolated into DEFAULT_URL_PATTERN (as defined in your Defaults.py file or
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] overridden by settings in mm_cfg.py).
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] Note that listnames are forced to lowercase.
[2025-07-09 03:13:36 +0000] Illegal list name: mailman@1bc2c01b8357

[2025-07-09 03:14:38 +0000] # mailman post: start
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] Create a new, unpopulated mailing list.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] Usage: /usr/local/cpanel/3rdparty/mailman/bin/newlist [options] [listname [listadmin-addr [admin-password]]]
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] Options:
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     -l language
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     --language=language
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         Make the list's preferred language `language', which must be a two
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         letter language code.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     -u urlhost
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     --urlhost=urlhost
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         Gives the list's web interface host name.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     -e emailhost
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     --emailhost=emailhost
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         Gives the list's email domain name.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     -q/--quiet
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         Normally the administrator is notified by email (after a prompt) that
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         their list has been created.  This option suppresses the prompt and
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         notification.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     -a/--automate
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         This option suppresses the prompt prior to administrator notification
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         but still sends the notification.  It can be used to make newlist
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         totally non-interactive but still send the notification, assuming
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         listname, listadmin-addr and admin-password are all specified on the
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         command line.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     -h/--help
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]         Print this help text and exit.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] You can specify as many of the arguments as you want on the command line:
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] you will be prompted for the missing ones.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] Every Mailman list has two parameters which define the default host name for
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] outgoing email, and the default URL for all web interfaces.  When you
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] configured Mailman, certain defaults were calculated, but if you are running
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] multiple virtual Mailman sites, then the defaults may not be appropriate for
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] the list you are creating.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] You also specify the domain to create your new list in by typing the command
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] like so:
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     newlist --urlhost=www.mydom.ain mylist
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] where `www.mydom.ain' should be the base hostname for the URL to this virtual
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] hosts's lists.  E.g. with this setting people will view the general list
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] overviews at http://www.mydom.ain/mailman/listinfo.  Also, www.mydom.ain
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] should be a key in the VIRTUAL_HOSTS mapping in mm_cfg.py/Defaults.py if
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] the email hostname to be automatically determined.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] If you want the email hostname to be different from the one looked up by the
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] VIRTUAL_HOSTS or if urlhost is not registered in VIRTUAL_HOSTS, you can specify
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] `emailhost' like so:
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     newlist --urlhost=www.mydom.ain --emailhost=mydom.ain mylist
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] where `mydom.ain' is the mail domain name. If you don't specify emailhost but
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] urlhost is not in the virtual host list, then mm_cfg.DEFAULT_EMAIL_HOST will
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] be used for the email interface.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] For backward compatibility, you can also specify the domain to create your
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] new list in by spelling the listname like so:
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]     mylist@www.mydom.ain
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] where www.mydom.ain is used for `urlhost' but it will also be used for
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] `emailhost' if it is not found in the virtual host table. Note that
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] '--urlhost' and '--emailhost' have precedence to this notation.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] If you spell the list name as just `mylist', then the email hostname will be
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] taken from DEFAULT_EMAIL_HOST and the url will be taken from DEFAULT_URL_HOST
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] interpolated into DEFAULT_URL_PATTERN (as defined in you
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] r Defaults.py file or
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] overridden by settings in mm_cfg.py).
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000] Note that listnames are forced to lowercase.
[2025-07-09 03:14:39 +0000]

[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     -l language
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     --language=language
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         Make the list's preferred language `language', which must be a two
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         letter language code.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     -u urlhost
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     --urlhost=urlhost
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         Gives the list's web interface host name.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     -e emailhost
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     --emailhost=emailhost
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         Gives the list's email domain name.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     -q/--quiet
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         Normally the administrator is notified by email (after a prompt) that
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         their list has been created.  This option suppresses the prompt and
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         notification.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     -a/--automate
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         This option suppresses the prompt prior to administrator notification
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         but still sends the notification.  It can be used to make newlist
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         totally non-interactive but still send the notification, assuming
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         listname, listadmin-addr and admin-password are all specified on the
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         command line.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     -h/--help
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]         Print this help text and exit.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] You can specify as many of the arguments as you want on the command line:
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] you will be prompted for the missing ones.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] Every Mailman list has two parameters which define the default host name for
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] outgoing email, and the default URL for all web interfaces.  When you
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] configured Mailman, certain defaults were calculated, but if you are running
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] multiple virtual Mailman sites, then the defaults may not be appropriate for
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] the list you are creating.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] You also specify the domain to create your new list in by typing the command
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] like so:
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     newlist --urlhost=www.mydom.ain mylist
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] where `www.mydom.ain' should be the base hostname for the URL to this virtual
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] hosts's lists.  E.g. with this setting people will view the general list
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] overviews at http://www.mydom.ain/mailman/listinfo.  Also, www.mydom.ain
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] should be a key in the VIRTUAL_HOSTS mapping in mm_cfg.py/Defaults.py if
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] the email hostname to be automatically determined.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] If you want the email hostname to be different from the one looked up by the
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] VIRTUAL_HOSTS or if urlhost is not registered in VIRTUAL_HOSTS, you can specify
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] `emailhost' like so:
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     newlist --urlhost=www.mydom.ain --emailhost=mydom.ain mylist
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] where `mydom.ain' is the mail domain name. If you don't specify emailhost but
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] urlhost is not in the virtual host list, then mm_cfg.DEFAULT_EMAIL_HOST will
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] be used for the email interface.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] For backward compatibility, you can also specify the domain to create your
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] new list in by spelling the listname like so:
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000]     mylist@www.mydom.ain
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] where www.mydom.ain is used for `urlhost' but it will also be used for
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] `emailhost' if it is not found in the virtual host table. Note that
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] '--urlhost' and '--emailhost' have precedence to this notation.
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] If you spell the list name as just `mylist', then the email hostname will be
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] taken from DEFAULT_EMAIL_HOST and the url will be taken from DEFAULT_URL_HOST
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] interpolated into DEFAULT_URL_PATTERN (as defined in your Defaults.py file or
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] overridden by settings in mm_cfg.py).
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] 
[2025-07-09 03:14:54 +0000] Note that listnames are forced to lowercase.
